methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $63M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Standard Bots, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $63M to date, is valued against a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company in the robotics sector. The peer set includes later-stage companies such as 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), and Dexterity ($1.6B), as well as earlier peers like Fairmat ($350M) and Collaborative Robotics ($500M).

Two valuation methods anchor the analysis. The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $63M total raised as a baseline proxy, implying a $120M valuation. The Peer-Median method derives a $1.1B implied value based on the median of six same-stage, same-sector peers, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer range reflects significant heterogeneity in robotics funding outcomes at Series B, driven by technology differentiation and market timing.

Triangulating these two methods produces a low estimate of $102M, a median of $597M, and a high of $1.2B. The median aligns closer to the peer-median result, reflecting the maturity of comparable Series B robotics companies. Key uncertainties include Standard Bots' specific technology defensibility relative to the heterogeneous peer set and near-term path to Series C, which materially influences stage-transition multiples. The valuation range of $600M–$1.2B represents conservative-to-market scenarios; the $102M floor reflects only stage-based progression and is likely understated given peer precedent.
